2013-08-01.title: Head of Syrian Opposition Rejects Talks With Assad
2013-08-01.abstract: Ahmad al-Jarba, president of the Syrian National Coalition, hardened his position and also said talks must be put off until the rebels made military gains.

2013-08-01.title: Despite West’s Efforts, Afghan Youths Cling to Traditional Ways
2013-08-01.abstract: While young Afghans have cultivated Western trappings like cellphones and jeans, they have resisted the West’s efforts to instill values like expanded rights for women.

2013-08-01.title: Embracing the West, Except Its Ideals
2013-08-01.abstract: Despite being exposed to Western culture for the last decade, a new generation of Afghans is clinging to their society’s conservative ways.

2013-08-01.title: Road Through Roman History Creates Colossal Headache
2013-08-01.abstract: A plan to ban private vehicles on Via dei Fori Imperiali, a wide, busy artery that passes the Colosseum, has pitted the mayor and conservationists against residents and shops.

2013-08-01.title: Lawmakers in Uruguay Vote to Legalize Marijuana
2013-08-01.abstract: Legislators approved the bill, opening the way for the authorities to create one of Latin America’s most ambitious nationwide endeavors in overhauling drug policy.

2013-08-02.title: Iraq: 1,057 Killed in July, U.N. Says
2013-08-02.abstract: The United Nations said July was Iraq’s deadliest month in more than five years, describing a series of bombings and shootings as an epidemic of sectarian-tinged violence.

2013-08-02.title: Terror Threat Prompts U.S. to Close Diplomatic Missions
2013-08-02.abstract: Officials gave few details about what prompted the temporary closings but cited “credible” information that an Al Qaeda affiliate might be plotting an attack.

2013-08-02.title: Iran Assails House Sanctions Bill, Saying It Will Complicate Nuclear Dispute
2013-08-02.abstract: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said the legislation passed by the House of Representatives on Wednesday would complicate efforts to settle the nuclear standoff diplomatically.

2013-08-02.title: A Culture Clings to Its Reflection in a Cleaned-Up Soap Opera
2013-08-02.abstract: “Baktun,” made in the Mayan language and set in the community, might be the cleanest telenovela in Mexico.

2013-08-02.title: Kurdish Struggle Blurs Syria’s Battle Lines
2013-08-02.abstract: Kurds are using the chaos in Syria to seek autonomy, but their struggle does not fit neatly into the war between President Bashar al-Assad and the rebels seeking his ouster.

2013-08-03.title: Spain: Alarms Warned Train’s Driver Before Accident
2013-08-03.abstract: The alarms went off inside the train that crashed last week in northwestern Spain moments before it derailed, prompting the panicked driver to activate the brakes three times, an examination revealed.

2013-08-03.title: In Mali, a Race Between a Former Finance Minister and a Pro-French Favorite
2013-08-03.abstract: Mali will hold a runoff on Aug. 11 between two presidential candidates, Ibrahim Boubacar Keita and Soumaila Cissé.

2013-08-03.title: Berlusconi’s Followers Threaten Fragile Truce in Italy
2013-08-03.abstract: Members of the former Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi’s party talked of mass resignations to protest his prison sentence for tax fraud.

2013-08-03.title: Egypt Says It May Shut Entrances to Camps
2013-08-03.abstract: As clashes between demonstrators and security forces continued in and near Cairo, the Egyptian government threatened to block entrances to a large protest camp.

2013-08-03.abstract: Intercepted electronic communications in which senior operatives of Al Qaeda discussed attacks in the Middle East and North Africa led the United States to issue a global travel alert to its citizens, officials said.

2013-08-04.title: Restraint Urged for Egypt’s Security Services
2013-08-04.abstract: Egypt’s interim vice president said on Saturday that force could be used only as a last resort to disperse demonstrations held by supporters of Mohamed Morsi.

2013-08-04.abstract: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, August 4

2013-08-04.title: Syria Bans Use of Foreign Currencies for Business Deals
2013-08-04.abstract: The ban, which carries fines and jail time for those who engage in such transactions without government approval, is an effort to prop up the Syrian pound, whose value has plummeted since 2011.

2013-08-04.title: Former Envoy to Syria Said to Be Choice for Cairo Post
2013-08-04.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry recommended Robert S. Ford, who most recently served as a link to the Syrian opposition in the civil war, officials said.

2013-08-04.title: Iranian President Is Sworn In and Presents a New Cabinet of Familiar Faces
2013-08-04.abstract: Many members of Hassan Rouhani’s cabinet, which must still be confirmed by Parliament, had served under a moderate former president.

2013-08-05.title: Hours to Go, Just to Get to Work
2013-08-05.abstract: With 9.9 million vehicles taking to the streets of Jakarta each workday, the daily commute can take a toll on stressed Indonesians.

2013-08-05.abstract: In South Korea, the government is playing a bigger role in matchmaking amid plunging birthrates and changing expectations over matrimony.

2013-08-05.abstract: For Palestinian young men and boys, throwing stones at Israeli soldiers and settlers is a rite of passage.

2013-08-05.abstract: Pending prosecutions for three officials are a new blow to the Muslim Brotherhood, which emerged as Egypt’s strongest political movement in 2011 but whose power has dissipated.

2013-08-05.abstract: The decision involving the settlements highlighted the fragility of the Washington-brokered effort to resume peace negotiations.

2013-08-06.title: Michael J. Morwood, Archaeologist Who Helped Find ‘New Human,’ Dies at 62
2013-08-06.abstract: In 2003, Dr. Morwood and his team dug up remains of Homo floresiensis, or the little people of Flores who were nicknamed the hobbits, in an Indonesian island cave.

2013-08-06.abstract: As the Syrian civil war rages, scores of Syrian casualties have been discreetly spirited across the hostile frontier for lifesaving treatment in Israel, an enemy country.

2013-08-06.title: A French Town Bridges the Gap Between Muslims and Non-Muslims
2013-08-06.abstract: Roubaix, a small city in northeastern France, has made a point of embracing its Muslim population, diminishing the ethnic and sectarian tensions that have afflicted other parts of France.

2013-08-06.abstract: The victory, said to have been led by two foreign men who carried out a suicide attack, was bound to boost the morale of an insurgency that has faltered.

2013-08-06.abstract: The villa is at the center of the criminal charges against Bo Xilai, a fallen Communist Party aristocrat, that include accusations of taking bribes from a young Chinese billionaire.

2013-08-07.title: Kremlin Critic Goes From Jail to Trail
2013-08-07.abstract: Aleksei A. Navalny, who was freed pending an appeal of his embezzlement conviction, has returned to a quixotic campaign to challenge the political system established by President Vladimir V. Putin.

2013-08-07.abstract: Humanitarian groups are warning of a looming disaster in the former French colony, where Seleka rebels have been looting, raping and killing, and coups have outnumbered fair elections.

2013-08-07.title: New Leaks Into Pacific at Japan Nuclear Plant
2013-08-07.abstract: Tons of contaminated groundwater are emptying into the ocean daily, but the operator of the stricken Fukushima plant has not identified the cause of the problem.

2013-08-07.abstract: Ayman al-Zawahri had been seen as ineffectual, but a secret correspondence with the Yemeni Qaeda affiliate leader suggests he might still bear major authority.

2013-08-07.title: 2 Senators Visit Egypt With Threat on U.S. Aid
2013-08-07.abstract: Senators John McCain and Lindsey Graham made the trip to Cairo at the request of President Obama.

2013-08-08.title: Venezuelan Court Rejects Challenge to Presidential Election Results
2013-08-08.abstract: The court also ordered Henrique Capriles, who lost to Nicolás Maduro, to pay a fine for insulting government authority by accusing the judicial system of bias.

2013-08-08.abstract: The decision to shut some American diplomatic posts in the Middle East has prompted gloating among jihadists and criticism by some officials who say the Obama administration overreacted.

2013-08-08.abstract: The Hong Kong-based company Li & Fung is a matchmaker between poor countries’ factories and affluent nations’ vendors. But critics call it the garment industry’s “sweatshop locator.”

2013-08-08.title: John Woodward, Leader of British Navy in Falkland Islands War, Dies at 81
2013-08-08.abstract: Admiral Woodward, as commander of a Royal Navy battle group in 1982, gave the order to sink the Argentine cruiser General Belgrano.

2013-08-09.title: As Foreign Fighters Flood Syria, Fears of a New Extremist Haven
2013-08-09.abstract: Extremists are carving out pockets of territory for Islamist militants, posing what intelligence officials say may become the greatest security threat in the world today.

2013-08-09.abstract: Four of the five top-ranked female distance race walkers in the world are from Saransk, an otherwise unremarkable city of about 310,000 people 320 miles southeast of Moscow.

2013-08-09.abstract: A previously undisclosed launching site and space center could theoretically be used for testing ballistic missiles, according to a report.

2013-08-09.abstract: Jaroslaw Kaczynski, the blunt and polarizing opposition leader, is being aided by a newly lackluster economy that has left more Poles longing for a strong, populist hand.

2013-08-10.title: Japan’s Debt Looks Like This: 1,000,000,000,000,000 Yen
2013-08-10.abstract: Japan’s soaring national debt has reached a new milestone, surpassing one quadrillion yen. That’s a one followed by 15 zeros. The mind boggles.

2013-08-10.title: Kenyan Inquiries Find No Sign of Explosion in Airport Fire, President Says
2013-08-10.abstract: President Uhuru Kenyatta said initial investigations had found that “this was a simple fire gone bad.”

2013-08-10.title: Inquiry Focuses on BBC Severance Payments
2013-08-10.abstract: The police in Britain opened an investigation after an audit found departing executives from the British Broadcasting Corporation received more than they were entitled to.

2013-08-10.title: Kerry and Hagel Meet With Their Russian Counterparts
2013-08-10.abstract: A day of high-level meetings yielded little definite accomplishments, underscoring the many unresolved remaining issues between the two countries.

2013-08-10.title: Chilean President Apologizes for Census
2013-08-10.abstract: President Sebastián Piñera apologized for serious mistakes in the nationwide census last year, which, despite intense efforts, failed to survey over 9 percent of the population.

2013-08-11.title: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, August 11
2013-08-11.abstract: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, August 11.

2013-08-11.title: New Israeli Housing Bids Raise Tensions Before Peace Talks
2013-08-11.abstract: A plan to build houses in contested East Jerusalem and West Bank settlements intensifies the already charged atmosphere, three days before the resumption of Israeli-Palestinian peace talks.

2013-08-11.abstract: Lon Snowden, Edward J. Snowden’s father, and the family’s lawyer indicated they would encourage a return to the United States, but only if acceptable trial conditions could be negotiated.

2013-08-11.abstract: Some citizens said the vote was proof that Mali, after 18 months of armed turmoil, was intent on righting itself and renewing a democratic tradition.

2013-08-12.abstract: Indian forces and local authorities enforced the curfews after rioting on Friday left two civilians dead.

2013-08-12.abstract: The American Embassy in Yemen remained closed, underscoring the challenges President Obama faces in trying to reshape the nation’s counterterrorism strategy.

2013-08-12.abstract: Kenneth Bae, 45, a tour operator who was sentenced to 15 years of hard labor, has left the camp where he was being held. His health has declined, his sister said.

2013-08-13.abstract: Between trips to medieval palaces and bridges, visitors to Prague can take a bus tour showcasing the country’s culture of lawlessness and corruption, and nibble on cookies labeled “presidential pardon.”

2013-08-13.abstract: The raids have reportedly ensnared nearly 1,500 foreigners, including detainees being held in a Moscow tent camp under conditions that Human Rights Watch called inhuman.

2013-08-13.abstract: Syrian rebels have found an unlikely source for arms in Sudan, whose government sold the weapons to Qatar, which arranged delivery through Turkey, Western officials and the rebels said.

2013-08-14.abstract: The move was part of a deal brokered by the United States allowing the resumption of peace talks on Wednesday.

2013-08-15.abstract: The military-backed government in Egypt had hinted at a milder clearing operation that would last days. But when it came, protesters appeared stunned by its fury.

2013-08-15.abstract: A highly disciplined dance boot camp has become one of the most successful programs for troubled youths in Britain.

2013-08-15.abstract: As part of an American-brokered deal, Israel released 26 Palestinian prisoners, spawning protests in Tel Aviv and celebrations in Ramallah.

2013-08-16.abstract: A Michael Jackson tribute show by Cirque du Soleil startled China’s capital with a brief flash of a censored 1989 photo of a man facing down tanks near Tiananmen.

2013-08-16.abstract: All of Hassan Rouhani’s key nominees were approved, most notably the foreign minister, an American-educated diplomat known for his understanding of the West.

2013-08-16.abstract: The Syrian war has proved to be a sanctuary for the group’s Iraq affiliates, drawing jihadi recruits and leading to a rise in suicide bombings in Iraq, American officials say.

2013-08-16.abstract: Many Egyptians say that the military had to crack down on pro-Morsi supporters and that the protesters were holding back the country with endless sit-ins.

2013-08-17.abstract: Hasan Nasrallah, the leader of Hezbollah, accused radical Sunnis of orchestrating a car bombing that killed 24 in Beirut.

2013-08-17.abstract: The ferocity of the attacks appears to have been a deliberate calculation of the military-appointed government to provoke violence from the Muslim Brotherhood and its allies, historians said Friday.

2013-08-18.title: North Korea Agrees to Talk About Reunions for Families

2013-08-18.abstract: Bo Xilai, an ambitious politician whose downfall shook the Communist Party elite, will face charges of bribery, corruption and abuse of power.

2013-08-18.abstract: An electoral court has removed the president, the wife of his longtime rival and a former president from its list of candidates, officials said.

2013-08-19.abstract: President Bashar al-Assad and rebels seeking to oust him have accused each other of deploying chemical weapons.

2013-08-19.abstract: Many Afghans who have grown cynical after years of war have found a voice in a sharp-tongued poet who fixes trucks by day and writes by night.

2013-08-19.abstract: An intensified campaign with foreign leaders and diplomats this week will press the case that there is no better hope to prevent further chaos.

2013-08-19.abstract: The State Department has put aid on hold for economic programs that directly involve the Egyptian government, but not the much larger military aid on which Egypt’s generals depend.

2013-08-20.abstract: South Korea and the United States began another round of military drills on Monday, but North Korea remained uncharacteristically silent.

2013-08-20.abstract: The commemoration of the bombing of the United Nations Baghdad offices was overshadowed by staff members who said internal labor practices had compromised their safety and security.

2013-08-20.abstract: In a nation used to 6 percent annual expansion, something closer to 5 percent, which would be a reason for celebration elsewhere, is producing angst.

2013-08-20.title: Uncertainty Over a Palace of Parking
2013-08-20.abstract: In a country with 1.3 million recognized memorials, historically protected buildings and sites, the preservation of a sooty parking garage covered with graffiti is a tough sell.

2013-08-20.title: Bo Guagua’s Statement
2013-08-20.abstract: Mr. Bo’s father, Bo Xilai, a former Chinese Communist Party official, is scheduled to go on trial on charges of taking bribes, corruption and abuse of power.

2013-08-21.title: Cairo Military Firmly Hooked to U.S. Lifeline
2013-08-21.abstract: The $1.3 billion in annual military aid to Egypt pays for the kind of weapons that its military loves, and may give America more leverage than it seems.

2013-08-21.abstract: At the Zeinhom morgue in Cairo, men who said they were neighborhood watchmen tried to keep bereaved families from telling their stories.

2013-08-21.abstract: China’s leaders are engaged in a delicate balancing act, with goals of both parading Mr. Bo as a criminal and also avoiding tarring the leftist policies he championed so as not to alienate important revolutionary families.

2013-08-21.title: South Sudan: Commander Arrested
2013-08-21.abstract: The president ordered the arrest of a high-ranking army commander over suspected violations of human rights, a military spokesman said Tuesday.

2013-08-21.abstract: Four officials who were placed on administrative leave after the deadly attack last year on the United States diplomatic post in Benghazi have been reinstated and given new assignments.

2013-08-22.abstract: Now that it has elected a new president, Mali’s greatest enemy may not be Muslim extremists, but the corruption that has lingered for decades.

2013-08-22.title: In Lebanon, Neighbor’s War Feels Like Its Own
2013-08-22.abstract: A bombing in Beirut that killed 27 people has many Lebanese fearing that the war in Syria could easily spill across the border.

2013-08-22.title: China Boss’s Fall Puts Focus on a Business Ally
2013-08-22.abstract: Chinese officials are relying on evidence from Xu Ming, a businessman with close ties to the former Politburo member Bo Xilai.

2013-08-22.title: General Says Syrian Rebels Aren’t Ready to Take Power
2013-08-22.abstract: The nation’s top military officer said that even if the Pentagon intervened in Syria, there were no moderate rebel groups ready to fill any power vacuum.

2013-08-22.abstract: India’s inability to pull hundreds of millions out of poverty despite decades of robust growth has sparked an unusually nasty fight between two of its greatest economists.

2013-08-23.title: These Walls Speak, Recalling Victims of Violence

2013-08-23.abstract: Officials are investigating whether the victims are a group of young people kidnapped from a Mexico City bar in May.

2013-08-23.abstract: Some are questioning whether Catherine M. Russell, ambassador at large for global women’s issues, will see a less prominent role under Secretary of State John Kerry.

2013-08-23.abstract: A parliamentary study found that prejudices played a role in the police’s failure to detect a neo-Nazi cell that carried out a string of attacks on immigrants for more than a decade.

2013-08-23.abstract: Iran put down asphalt over large sections of a military base, satellite images show, suggesting an attempt to sanitize a site where United Nations inspectors have tried to gain access.

2013-08-24.title: Nuclear Operator Raises Alarm on Crisis
2013-08-24.abstract: Tepco, the operator of Japan’s tsunami-hit nuclear power plant, said there were more than 200,000 tons of radioactive water in tanks vulnerable to leaks, adding to a list of problems.

2013-08-24.abstract: Joaquim Barbosa is the driving force behind a series of establishment-shaking rulings, turning Brazil’s highest court — and him in particular — into a newfound political power.

2013-08-24.abstract: President Obama’s national security aides are studying the NATO air war in Kosovo as a possible blueprint for acting without a mandate from the United Nations.

2013-08-24.abstract: Mr. Christian, whose great-great-great-grandfather led the mutiny on the Bounty in 1789 and sought refuge on Pitcairn Island, kept the isle connected to the world.

2013-08-24.abstract: One million children are now refugees from the conflict raging in Syria, the United Nations reported Friday, calling it a “shameful milestone” in a crisis now well into its third year.

2013-08-25.title: Battling Superstition, Indian Paid With His Life
2013-08-25.abstract: The killing of Narendra Dabholkar, a former physician who aimed to drive skepticism into India’s superstition, is the latest episode in a wrestling match between traditionalists and reformers.

2013-08-25.abstract: A look at other Muslim countries that have struggled with democratic transitions provides a kind of warning to Egypt’s military leaders.

2013-08-25.title: Grim World of Mumbai Rape Accused
2013-08-25.abstract: Family members and neighbors of two of the five suspects react to the alleged involvement of the young men in the rape of a 22-year-old photojournalist.

2013-08-25.title: Political Staging in Trial of Fallen China Official
2013-08-25.abstract: What has most captivated ordinary Chinese — thanks to headlines in major state media outlets — is a mountain of testimony that depicts Mr. Bo as the archetypal corrupt official.

2013-08-25.title: Two More Arrests Made in Gang Rape in India
2013-08-25.abstract: The five suspects are accused of assaulting a 22-year-old photojournalist on assignment for an English-language magazine.

2013-08-26.title: From Italy, a Vintage Redolent of Horrors
2013-08-26.abstract: The Simon Wiesenthal Center has called on distributors to stop handling a line of Italian wines that carry labels that portray Hitler and sundry members of the Nazi hierarchy.

2013-08-26.title: Surveillance Revelations Shake U.S.-German Ties
2013-08-26.abstract: The issue is prompting not just a debate about privacy and data protection, but also demands from German officials that the Berlin-Washington security partnership be put on a new footing.

2013-08-26.title: Activist Running for Mayor of Moscow Is Briefly Detained
2013-08-26.abstract: Aleksei A. Navalny was held by the police after staging a rally that attracted several thousand supporters.

2013-08-26.title: As Egyptians Ignore Curfew, Talk of a U.S.-Brotherhood Conspiracy
2013-08-26.abstract: Among supporters of the military, which ousted Mohamed Morsi, there is a widespread view that the United States and the Muslim Brotherhood have a plan to destroy Egypt.

2013-08-26.title: Egypt Military Enlists Religion to Quell Ranks
2013-08-26.abstract: Egyptian military leaders are worried about insubordination among the rank and file after members of the security forces killed hundreds of their fellow countrymen.

2013-08-27.title: Party Leader in India Becomes Ill After Speech
2013-08-27.abstract: Sonia Gandhi, 66, whose health has been a subject of speculation, left a parliamentary session and was admitted to a cardiology unit.

2013-08-27.title: Seeking Asylum in Australia, Refugees Find a Policy in Flux
2013-08-27.abstract: Accounts from some who have fled their countries for Australia describe dangerous sea voyages that can lead to deportment to Papua New Guinea and long waits for resettlement.

2013-08-27.title: Gorky Park, Once Drab, Now Glows
2013-08-27.abstract: The park has transformed over the past two years into an extravagant urban recreational space that offers Muscovites a respite from the intense political pressures of Russia.

2013-08-27.title: Areas Affected by the Alleged Chemical Attack in Syria
2013-08-27.abstract: Opposition groups said that rockets carrying chemical weapons hit the towns of Ain Tarma, Zamalka, Jobar and Muadamiya shortly after 2 a.m. Wednesday. Victims were taken to clinics in neighboring towns.

2013-08-27.title: Blasts in the Night, a Smell, and a Flood of Syrian Victims
2013-08-27.abstract: The attack has brought widespread condemnation on President Bashar al-Assad’s government, and it is shaping up as an inflection point for a war that has been grinding on for more than two years.

2013-08-28.title: U.S. Officials Return to Pursuit of 1985 Killer of American Agent
2013-08-28.abstract: The early release from prison of the killer of Enrique Camarena had D.E.A. officials asking, “Who dropped the ball?”

2013-08-28.title: Made in Lab, Fleeting Element May Join Periodic Table
2013-08-28.abstract: Physicists at Lund University in Sweden led the work to confirm the decade-old discovery of an element by creating a new atom that lasted less than a second.

2013-08-28.title: Tunisia Broadens Crackdown on Extremists
2013-08-28.abstract: The prime minister says Ansar al-Shariah was behind two political assassinations this year.

2013-08-28.title: Salvadoran Linked to Killing to Serve Time in U.S. Prison
2013-08-28.abstract: A former colonel convicted of immigration violations in the United States, and suspected in the 1989 killing of six priests in El Salvador, could face trial in Spain.

2013-08-28.title: Reports of Syria Chemical Attack Spur Question: Why?
2013-08-28.abstract: Defenders of Syria’s president, Bashar al-Assad said he would not have benefited from launching any chemical attack, but even his accusers puzzled over a motive.

2013-08-29.title: Press Adds ‘What if?’ to Five W’s in France
2013-08-29.abstract: With vacationing readers less inclined to read hard news in the summer, French newspapers and magazines have left some of the news behind in favor of far more creative writing.

2013-08-29.title: Iran Slows Its Gathering of Uranium, Report Says
2013-08-29.abstract: International nuclear inspectors said that Iran was slowing its accumulation of uranium that could be quickly turned into fuel for an atom bomb.

2013-08-29.title: U.S. Military Deaths in Afghanistan
2013-08-29.abstract: The Department of Defense has identified 2,249 American service members who have died as a part of the Afghan war and related operations.

2013-08-29.title: U.S. Facing Test on Data to Back Action on Syria
2013-08-29.abstract: The Obama administration says the information it will make public will show proof of a large-scale chemical attack by Syrian forces.

2013-08-29.title: Amid Chaos, Israelis Take a Stoic View
2013-08-29.abstract: Many Israelis said this summer had spawned an “I-told-you-so” sensibility among those skeptical about the Arab Spring, and a sense of relief that for now, the problems are elsewhere.

2013-08-30.title: Taliban Attack Prompts Call for Coalition Air Support
2013-08-30.abstract: The battle was latest in a string of violent episodes in recent weeks on the country’s western edge.

2013-08-30.title: As Floods Ravage Sudan, Young Volunteers Revive a Tradition of Aid
2013-08-30.abstract: As the Sudanese government has been slow to respond, critics say, the members of Nafeer, a volunteer youth-led initiative, have taken phone calls and headed out in trucks to deliver bags of aid.

2013-08-30.title: Aim of U.S. Attack: Restore a ‘Red Line’ That Became Blurred
2013-08-30.abstract: The military strategy that the Obama administration is considering is not linked to its larger diplomatic strategy of persuading President Bashar al-Assad of Syria to yield power.

2013-08-30.title: In Thailand, Rubber Price Plunge Has Political Cost
2013-08-30.abstract: After falling rubber prices drove many rubber farmers in south Thailand into debt, hundreds of them protested in a clash that has helped embolden political opposition.

2013-08-30.title: Looming Airstrikes in Syria Pose Test for Egypt’s Leaders and the Opposition
2013-08-30.abstract: Egyptians braced for the ninth weekend of protests as the looming possibility of Western airstrikes against Syria injected a new element of volatility onto the streets.

2013-08-31.title: Fallen Official’s Defense: He Was Obeying Orders
2013-08-31.abstract: Bo Xilai said at his trial that he had been ordered to cover up his police chief’s flight to an American Consulate, according to a court observer’s record.

2013-08-31.title: Signs of a Shift Among Egyptian Protesters to Antigovernment, From Pro-Morsi
2013-08-31.abstract: At protests on Friday, there was less talk of restoring the ousted president and more denunciation of tactics used against Muslim Brotherhood supporters.

2013-08-31.title: Britain Cites Grave Risks in Leaked Data It Seized
2013-08-31.abstract: A court extended police powers to analyze data taken from David Miranda, whose partner, Glenn Greenwald, has led the reporting on material leaked by Edward J. Snowden.

2013-08-31.title: Support Slipping, U.S. Defends Plan for Syria Attack
2013-08-31.abstract: Facing faltering support in foreign capitals and Congress, the Obama administration made an aggressive push to justify military intervention in Syria on the grounds that American credibility was at stake.

2013-08-31.title: In Turnaround, It’s France Backing Arms While Britain Sits on Syria Sidelines
2013-08-31.abstract: In contrast to the 2003 Iraq war, France is asserting its power and Britain is stepping back, raising questions about whether and how much the European strategic landscape has changed since then.
